title: Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors
subtitle: Net [official development assistance](#dod:oda) to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Net official development development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors

Net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors. This data is expressed in US dollars. It is adjusted for inflation but does not account for differences in living costs between countries.

The choropleth map displays net official development assistance to medical research and basic health sectors for the year 2023, with values expressed in constant 2022 US dollars. The visualization uses a color gradient ranging from pale yellow for zero dollars to dark blue for amounts exceeding 300 million dollars to represent the volume of aid received by different nations. Countries in North America, Western Europe, and Australia are shaded with diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for these regions. In contrast, large portions of Africa and Asia show varying levels of assistance. A cluster of countries in East and Southern Africa, including Ethiopia, Tanzania, and Mozambique, along with Pakistan in South Asia, are colored in the darkest blue shade, signifying that they each received more than 300 million dollars. Surrounding these high-value recipients, nations such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and India appear in medium-to-dark blue tones, placing their assistance levels generally between 30 million and 300 million dollars. Much of South America and Central Asia is depicted in lighter shades of green and teal, corresponding to the lower bands of the legend, specifically between 0 and 30 million dollars. Several countries in North Africa and the Middle East are shown in pale yellow or light green, indicating values near the bottom of the scale or at zero.
